steps.title: Identify the paradox signal
steps.content: The word although sets up a paradox: water is critical to life, but during early Earth when oceans dominated, water somehow affected protein formation in the opposite way. The blank must name that negative effect.

steps.title: Find the word for that effect
steps.content:
The blank needs to name a negative effect water had on protein formation — the paradox is that water usually enables life but blocked it here. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — exploited: "water likely exploited the formation of proteins" — exploited means took advantage of. This implies water benefited from or used protein formation, which is incoherent. ✗
  • Choice B — imitated: "water likely imitated the formation of proteins" — imitated means copied. Water mimicking protein formation makes no sense in this context. ✗
  • Choice C — hampered: "water likely hampered the formation of proteins" — hampered means hindered or obstructed. This captures the paradox: water blocked the very biomolecules it normally enables. ✓
  • Choice D — reflected: "water likely reflected the formation of proteins" — reflected means mirrored or showed. This doesn't express any obstruction or interference. ✗
That matches Choice C — hampered.
